title: Entropy
description: Learn entropy as an alternative impurity measure for classification splits.
takeaways: Entropy measures uncertainty in a node.
Like Gini, lower entropy means a purer node.
Decision trees can use entropy instead of Gini to guide splits.

practiceLab.objective: Compute Shannon entropy safely for zero probabilities, then compare entropy with Gini on pure, mixed, and even nodes.
instructions: Run the imports/proportions cell for the 6/4 label array.
Run the guided entropy function and confirm Entropy (6/4) is about 0.971.
Run the Gini comparison cell for the same labels.
Complete Exercise 1 for pure and even nodes.
Confirm entropy prints 0.000 for pure and 1.000 for 5/5.
Complete Exercise 2 to print a table of entropy and Gini for 6/4, pure, and 5/5.
Check that both measures are 0 on the pure node.
Answer the reflection questions about log(0) and information gain.
hints: Skip probabilities where p == 0 before calling math.log2.
Entropy for two classes peaks at 1.0 when proportions are 0.5/0.5.
Reuse the same label arrays when comparing entropy and Gini.
practiceLab.expectedOutput: Entropy ≈ 0.971 for 6/4, entropy 0.000 / 1.000 for pure / even, and a three-row entropy-vs-gini table.
